To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $I$ is the identity matrix and $\lambda$ is a scalar variable.

The given matrix is:
$$ A = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} -4 & -13 & 10 \\ -9 & 10 & 4 \\ 12 & -5 & -14 \end{array}\right) $$

The characteristic polynomial $p(\lambda)$ is defined as:
$$ p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I) = \det \left(\begin{array}{ccc} -4-\lambda & -13 & 10 \\ -9 & 10-\lambda & 4 \\ 12 & -5 & -14-\lambda \end{array}\right) $$

Let's expand this determinant along the first row:
$$ p(\lambda) = (-4-\lambda) \left[ (10-\lambda)(-14-\lambda) - (4)(-5) \right] - (-13) \left[ (-9)(-14-\lambda) - (4)(12) \right] + 10 \left[ (-9)(-5) - (10-\lambda)(12) \right] $$

**Step 1: Simplify the first term**
$$ M_{11} = (10-\lambda)(-14-\lambda) - (4)(-5) $$
$$ = -(10-\lambda)(14+\lambda) + 20 $$
$$ = -(140 + 10\lambda - 14\lambda - \lambda^2) + 20 $$
$$ = -(140 - 4\lambda - \lambda^2) + 20 $$
$$ = -140 + 4\lambda + \lambda^2 + 20 $$
$$ = \lambda^2 + 4\lambda - 120 $$

Now multiply by the cofactor $(-4-\lambda)$:
$$ T_1 = (-4-\lambda)(\lambda^2 + 4\lambda - 120) $$
$$ = -(\lambda+4)(\lambda^2 + 4\lambda - 120) $$
$$ = -(\
